reviews.review_description:
I'm going to hold back on giving this five stars for now, but I have very few complaints.
1. Several small campaigns is actually very fun but they generally have three objectives that are relatively easy to do if you know what you're doing.
2. Small amount of provinces to conquer. I don't actually hate this.
3. It might just be me but I've never had the enemy clans attack my provinces.
4. Can't pick where your units start before a battle, I've lost a few battles because of this but otherwise it's not really bad since it makes you have to think on the fly even more.
This is a fun mix of a simplified civilization meets age of empires. If you enjoy those then this will be worth picking up as well
doomstacking is a bit of a problem if you don't engage with the game's mechanics regardless on how much you dome stack you'll lose
This game is honestly amazing and the gameplay is extremely unique there’s no other game like it the only possible issue I see with it is that the gameplay does become repetitive after a while.

You are a daimyo in feudal Japan working to lead your clan to victory.  This is an awesome strategy game with solid balanced mechanics, great tactical turn-based combat, and excellent voice acting.  While usually in VR I try to do things you couldn’t do with flat-gaming, this one somehow transcends the medium.  What I mean is, this could easily be a flat game and it would be just as good.  But it’s just as good in VR too!  Very addicting, maybe as addictive as Civ although it’s definitely simpler and has a shorter progression, the combat is more tactical.  It’s easy to spend hours on this one, to the point where I have to avoid playing it sometimes later at night so I’m not staying up later than I should.  Definitely give this one a shot - it could easily become one of your go-to’s!

reviews.review_title: Best VR game I have ever played
reviews.review_description: 10 out 10 game was really fun! As someone with motion sickness this game was pretty nice. I do wish there was more campaign or something but It was super fun, I would say this well worth over 10 bucks. I personally would have got around 20. Love this game please more!!!

Good turn based combat, troop selection and leveling.
Simple limited resource economy to manage.
Limited army size and small maps, but fun.
Wanted to leave a review for a decent game.
You can't not have fun when playing this game, even when you are losing your still having a sh*t load of fun
I love this game, it's has a great balance between challenge and fun and is really immersive.

reviews.review_title: Hope Japanese text can be added
reviews.review_description:
Great game!
I really want to keep playing, but after the first battle I couldn’t figure out what to do next.

I am over 20 missions from 45.
There is one special hint, that you can win any campaing in 1 or 2 months.
Unbelievable? But it is true!
Build the cheapest army as new, repeate it, if you need, you can move units under another general and dismiss general -> so you have a free place for new army.
There is one bug: You finished all clan goals in 1 monthSSS! Instead month. ;)

It is a very simple but fun and enjoyable game.  I completely lost the track of time, I really advise to buy this game. I express hope of it becoming more complex. The only thing which I didn't like is the lack of manual saves (in most cases you won't need them, but still, it's a strategy).
Great game but playing with friends would make it 10 times better.

I am enjoying the game a lot so far, played through two of the first campaigns now. I especially love that the audio is in Japanese, it definitely lends itself to the immersion. One feature that either I can’t figure out or is missing which I feel should be included is the ability to place your army before the battle starts. By default when you start a battle your units randomly spawn on the battle field and you have to spend your first few turns arranging them as the enemy charges you.
Overall though I am very happy with the game, love the visuals and am enjoying the simplified(in comparison to similar pc games) tactical gameplay, I also enjoy that its turn based since I prefer time to think out my moves as opposed to real time strategy games

It took me a few restarts to figure out exactly how it works but now I've got hours into the first campaign. Really fun and keeps me coming back. I remember playing a game similar to this years ago and now that it is in VR it's even better. Yes, I can see it getting repetitive (the intellignece of the enemy is low, variety is limited, and the challenges are basically resource management) but I'm loving it so far.

This is a polished turn-based strategy game that is a unique niche-filler in my library. There is a good amount of campaign content (although I'd love to see more!) and the gameplay is highly addictive.

There are a few campaigns that seem nearly impossible to complete, and unfortunately the variation in possible strategies is pretty limited due to the simplicity of the game design. I'll keep on hammering away at those.

Overall, an easy buy.
A really good tactical game. Lots of replayability too!

Firstly it’s vastly undervalued, Worth far more than its current price, Let alone on offer. It takes you back to the medieval times of Japan and Shogunate. Allowing you to control the clans from those times and the Lords themselves.

You get totally lost in there, Organising your troops, Building provinces, Trading. Just incredibly well done and a brilliant strategy game.

My partner being Japanese herself, Also finds it interesting and if anything, Has lost her time with me as I’m too busy in this realm! 

Looks and plays fantastic, It’s worth its high rating, And worth alot more in value!

For context I’m a 49 year old man who’s enjoyed gaming for years. Cut back a fair bit with kids and work but really enjoying Shogun Empire 😁

I’ve never played the total war series. For me it’s sort of like a simpler age of empires 2 (which I loved years ago) in VR. Not as involved as that but there’s a fair bit going on. It’s a great mix of pick up and play while also having some depth to it. There’s some challenge and nuance to the gameplay which means you have to think. The benefit of course is the satisfaction of finally beating that castle level with your hobbled together force!

You can play while seated (or standing). The point being this is a physically relaxed VR game in contrast to some of the more physical. 

My 13 year old is now also playing and loving it. Definitely recommended

If the developers are reading; this in multiplayer or co-op would be brilliant!!
I think this is a great strategy game and a lot of fun, and it has a lot of potential.
Simple, immersive, strategic and fun. Maybe a little easy once you figure out the mechanics but you can spend many hours on this hidden gem, highly recommend!

I've got about 160 games on quest. For whatever reason this has got me in and is my most played game. It's great for pre bed chillout gaming. Would love to see a sequel that further develops the mechanics.
Enjoyed to play total war back in time. This game reminds me of the old days. Really good game. It's worth every penny

I am simply enjoying my time with warplanes WW1 then I saw the ad of this game in the menu. Saw the videos and youtube gameplay. Got my interest. Bought it. Played it and it was sooo awesome! As a total war fan. This the closest thing I can play shogun total war in VR. The immersion is great. The daimyo room, scrolls, economics, rock paper scissor system, battle movements and scenarios are amazing! Kudos to this team  in creating a game like this in VR. Please create a Roman, Medieval, Napoleonic Eras next. Collaborate with Total War if you need to haha! Simply amazing! Congrats guys!
What is the point of outnumbering and out leveling your opponent if it does you no good.
Easily my favourite game on VR for pick up and play and without being overcomplicated. A great way to spend time. Well worth the purchase.

This game really cool because you get to control your clans territory’s and eventually conquer japan. Ive even played all 3 of the warplanes games and they’re super fun. Once I saw the advertisement for this game I had to get it. Also this game also teaches you the importance of saving because you have to save money and rice to be able to feed your army and buy new units. I feel like a game that I would really liked them to make, is a game kind of like shoguns empire, but about Aztecs and you have to the Spanish and other tribes.

I just bought this game and it’s the kind of hex strategy games I’ve been looking for. I’m shocked it’s not at the top of the lists for popular and high rated games. It is very well put together, especially for the price. I would have paid $25 for it. The battle strategy scenes are impressive and very satisfying. The game is as simple or complex as you make it for yourself. The instructions and tutorial are very well done. It is spoken in Japanese with English subtitles but to me that just adds to the theme and authenticity of the game. You can build large armies, alliances,  it, sell, and grow rice. This game is very well thought out, obviously well tested, and although it was hard for me to find. I’m so glad I did!

Great game for fans of Total War with a turn base twist. Terrific gameplay, style, and graphics. Great balance of fighting, buying/selling rice, completing tasks and orders from the Emperor, and using your troops and terrain to your benefit. My only gripe is that the game becomes too easy once you unlock the long range riflemen with armor. You can literally just get 3-6 of them with your commander and dominate the battles. Would love to see an update for disabling troops or challenges to make the game more difficult or set it periods where only certain troops would be available. Also, maybe some historical battles scenarios where you choose a side and see how you would do with what you're given.
Cool, but not for me. I want to chill / relax, not read and think... but well made!
Well worth a buy! can’t wait to see how the game develops.

After playing for hours I needed to re-review this awsome game. 

This is a strategic hex game that is very similar too Romance of the 3 kindoms resource game. This isn’t for everyone , you actually HAVE to think to manage the game.  That being said…

Follow the tutorial, several times if needed. 

Tips:

All generals, troops all use rice. This is critical. 
Upgrade all your towns to produce rice. Concentrate-on rice. 
When rice prices are high, sell rice. 
When rice is low, buy rice.
Whenever possible ask for peace and relations eventually buying the province. (They will help you with larger battles but will cost.)
Get an upgrade as soon as possible the higher end cannon and the rockets, rifles are fun. 
Troops upgrade if you use them in battle AND you win. You can pay to upgrade but it’s not needed. 
Always try to defeat the general first if possible. Gieshas are good to do this before the battle even starts. Get her a soon as possible. 
Retreating enemies will join other clans. If you can eliminate as many as possible. Future battles will be less enemy’s. 

Tons more but you can figure out the rest. 

Don’t think, just buy it!

Devs- perfect game! But PLEASE add multiplayer player online battles! So much potential here for that!
I really like it, but I can't seem to scroll up or down when trying to look at the scrolls (example: monthly reports). If this can be fixed, that would be perfect!

developer_response.body: To scroll, please use the right analog thumbstick on your touch controller and move it in the up/down direction.

reviews.review_description: Seriously great combo of CIV and Total war gaming. The early to mid tech is really well done but I feel the late game tech is out of wack in comparison. Definitely recommend picking the game up!

I’m hesitant to buy a strategy game in VR (aside from Demeo) but this turned out to be a hidden gem. It is great and challenging, exactly the kind of stuff you need in a turn-based strategy game. The graphics is also great!
I really enjoy this game; it’s a lot of fun! However, it’s a pity that it doesn’t currently support Chinese. It would be fantastic if Chinese language support could be added in the future!

I really looked long time for a playable strategy game. Shogun filled this gap now. It has a map like the risk boardgame and your task in sandbox games is to conquer the world. This is done by building and managing your provinces, defend them, build armys and conquer other provinces. it has a free trial for 30 minutes. The controls are intuitive, the graphics great and all gameplay big fun. Complexity is medium to low, but you can play campaigns with more challenges or chose a higher difficulty setting. For the price it is a steal!

Homenet games makes the War Planes series, so I was curious to see what they would do here.  This is actually a well thought out turn based combat strategy game.  You can choose peace or invade through war.  So far it has plenty of campaigns to play through and after a few hours in it, I felt confident enough to recommend it.

reviews.review_title: A favorite of mine since a board game.
reviews.review_description: This game dropped quietly and I got it as soon as I was aware of it. I played Shogun as a board game 30+ years ago with friends, then I played the Total War version on my computer and it was my favorite game but only single player. Now this has become my favorite game in VR and I spend many hours in it. The one thing that would make it the all time best game would be if it were multiplayer.

reviews.review_title: Love it - please add other skins
reviews.review_description: Great work devs! Love these types of VR games. Easy win for the developer would be adding other skins/ scenarios such as medieval, modern combat, fantasy etc. Use the same engine but please give us dlc. Happy to pay. You already have a 2d fantasy version on Steam so it shouldn’t be too hard to transfer that over to vr.
It's a fun little game before you know it 3-6 hours have gone by, easily one of the best games on the quest

reviews.review_title: Love me some turn based tactics
reviews.review_description: If you're into medieval turn based strategy with some good challenges, this is a good choice.
There is something special in having the whole battlefield in VR, amazing game!
I love real time strategy turn based games. This is an underrated  game for sure. So much do do in the game . Graphics are good. Game play is good 👍 👌.
This game is fantastic.  There is nothing else like it on Quest and needs to be in the main store for sure!

Been playing on and off a few days straight. Can’t beat the price for such a quality game.

The camera feels a little wonky at times. Beyond that, love it.

reviews.review_title: A nice nod to the Total War series
reviews.review_description: This game is definitely inspired by Shogun Total war. This game is a lot of fun but I'm pretty bad at it. If you're looking for a game where you can play while relaxing on your couch then look no further.

reviews.review_title: Great execution of Turn Based Strategy
reviews.review_description: Shogun’s Empire proves once again that turn based and real time strategy games work so well in VR. Having a blast playing through the different scenarios. A great game for strategy game fans

reviews.review_title: More people need to play this!
reviews.review_description: This game is a great example of a strategy game in VR! Total War in VR! This game has so much content in it. I just spent 5 hours in it, and I've barely scratched this game so far. The voice acting is great! Please have people promote your game cause this game needs the recognition it deserves. Great job by the Warplane devs on this amazing game!
The game was so cool and I recommend buying it

reviews.review_title: Amazing Game! Can't wait for the next 1!
reviews.review_description: If you loved any of the Total War of Age of Empires games, you'll LOVE this game. Closest one I can find. Amazing Game!!

Played the full free trial and couldn't wait to have a little extra cash laying around to pick this up. I've been playing almost everyday for a week or more. I love building up my armies to conquer the map! Very good game, great animations, tactical driven, and very easy to learn but hard to master. Highly recommend getting this one
It’s not only a beautiful game, but fun and engaging. More value than games 4x the price; no regrets at all.
Best strategy vr, a lot of units, many campanies good graphics,

For $10 game this is masterfully made. Honestly, they could charge 20 and I would still feel satisfied. Charming as all hell, decent AI opponents, and so easy to learn. The only thing that could make this better for me would be a-reality update
